source.name: Windows path handling incident
source.license_notes: Original local workflow note from observed Claude Code path error.

goal: Avoid Windows path corruption when agents run commands through Bash or mixed shells.
when_to_use: Python cannot open a file because backslashes disappeared
steps: Detect whether the tool is Bash, PowerShell, or cmd.
In Bash/Git Bash, use forward slashes such as E:/DataBase/scripts/ui_design/search_ui.py or /e/DataBase where supported.
In PowerShell, native Windows paths with backslashes are safe.
Quote paths with spaces and avoid unescaped backslashes in Bash.
If path corruption occurs, rerun the command with forward slashes.
prompt_contract: Do not use raw Windows backslash paths in Bash commands.
Prefer local API calls over direct script paths when the API is running.
verification: No duplicated path fragments such as DataBaseDataBasescripts
Python script starts or API responds
failure_modes: Backslashes are consumed as escape characters
Drive path is treated as relative

quality.notes: Claude Code failed with E:\DataBase\DataBasescriptsui_designsearch_ui.py due to Bash backslash handling.

retrieval.ai_summary: On Windows, agents using Bash must avoid raw backslash paths. Use E:/DataBase/... or PowerShell. Prefer the local HTTP API to avoid path handling entirely.
